recommendations: Add a minimal automated test suite (unit tests for `modules/empyrical` metrics and smoke tests for each `Backtest_*` endpoint)
Create a `requirements.txt` (or lock file) aligned with `pyproject.toml` and pin key versions for reproducibility
Add CI workflow steps to run lint + tests on pull requests (current workflow appears publish-focused only)
Refactor `EigenLedger/main.py` (very large single file) into smaller domain modules to improve maintainability and MCP stability
Define explicit CLI entry points in `pyproject.toml` (e.g.
`eigenledger=EigenLedger.run:main`) instead of relying on `python -m ...`
Add input/output schemas and validation (Pydantic models) for all MCP-exposed endpoints to prevent runtime type issues
Prioritize import-safe API boundaries (avoid side effects at import time) since adapter mode is `import` with medium intrusiveness risk
Add structured logging and error taxonomy across backtest workflows for easier debugging in MCP service mode
Add performance benchmarks for heavy paths (`Backtest_Multiprocess`
`Backtest_Optimize`
visualization/dashboard generation) and track regressions
Document a stable public API contract separating core backtest orchestration from internal utilities (`roll/up/down`)
Strengthen packaging metadata (classifiers
license clarity
Python version constraints) and include optional dependency groups for plotting/dashboard features
Add security and quality checks (ruff/flake8
mypy
bandit
pip-audit) in CI
Create deterministic sample datasets and golden-result tests for analytics outputs (Sharpe
Sortino
VaR/CVaR
perf attribution)
Improve README with quickstart for both library and MCP usage
including end-to-end examples and expected outputs
Add cross-platform multiprocessing safeguards and tests (especially for Windows spawn behavior)
Set up release/versioning automation (semantic versioning + changelog) to support reliable downstream MCP integrations

success_factors: Workflow completed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize) with overall status=success.
Import-based adapter generation succeeded and produced runnable MCP scaffolding with healthy service status over stdio.
Repository structure was analyzable despite fallback ingestion method (zip_fallback), enabling endpoint discovery.
Generated plugin included broad endpoint coverage across orchestration and analytics functions.
failure_reasons: Quality validation gap: original project tests were not executed successfully (passed=false, no test files), reducing confidence in behavioral correctness.
DeepWiki analysis failed, limiting external architectural/context enrichment.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repo imported via zip fallback, indicating resilient retrieval path but potentially slower/less rich metadata than native clone.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with medium-confidence import feasibility (0.74); static analysis identified core modules/endpoints effectively.
node_performance.generation_time: Successful MCP code generation with required files and endpoint mapping; however reported LOC/size metrics are zero, indicating telemetry/reporting inconsistency.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed, but original project test signal is effectively absent; end-to-end correctness remains partially unverified.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Insufficient measured data (reported 0 MB). Likely acceptable for this run, but instrumentation is missing.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Insufficient measured data (reported 0%). Cannot evaluate true compute efficiency.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Generated artifact footprint appears small, but reported size=0 suggests metric collection issue rather than true zero usage.
